hawkish_phrases: corporate debt, and CRE valuations were elevated
high levels of corporate indebtedness and weak underwriting standards in leveraged loan markets
tight labor markets have in the past been associated with economic and financial imbalances
inflation modestly exceeding 2 percent for a period would be consistent
capital levels at U.S. banks were quite high relative to other sectors
dovish_phrases: overall inflation and inflation for items other than food and energy were running below 2 percent
business fixed investment and exports remained weak
inflation had averaged less than 2 percent over the past several years even as resource utilization had increased
downward pressures from global or technology-related factors that could continue to suppress inflation
the actual level of employment might still be below maximum employment
summary: The Committee voted unanimously to hold the target range at 1-1/2 to 1-3/4 percent with genuinely balanced language, noting strong labor markets alongside below-target inflation and weak business investment, and viewing the current stance as appropriate for a time provided the outlook remains broadly consistent.
